summary: Comprehensive backend + frontend testing of SignalRank resume screening MVP. 19/19 backend pytest tests passed, all key UI flows (dashboard, candidates list, candidate detail with 8-category breakdown + decision buttons, settings tabs with sliders/thresholds/keyword chips, save+rescore, reset) verified end-to-end against live backend with 6 seeded candidates.

issues: Row hit-area is limited - only the candidate name <Link> navigates to detail; clicking elsewhere in the row (email/filename/status/score cells) does nothing. Recruiter UX would benefit from making the entire row clickable (wrap row in Link or add onClick to <tr>).

action_items: Optional UX: make entire candidate row clickable (currently only name link navigates).
Optional: consider replacing window.confirm() in Settings reset with a styled shadcn AlertDialog for design consistency.
critical_code_review_comments: Backend server.py: route ordering is correct (/resumes/stats, /resumes/export, /resumes/rescore declared before /resumes/{candidate_id} — good).
MongoDB _id is properly excluded everywhere (find({}, {'_id': 0}) projections used consistently).
FastAPI on_event('shutdown') is deprecated in favor of lifespan handlers but functional. Minor.

scoring.py 'ai' substring check at line 131 will match many false positives (e.g. 'available', 'detailed', 'maintain'). Consider word-boundary regex. This may explain seeded scores skewing slightly high in AI category.
scoring.py 'cv' substring at line 125 will match 'cv2', 'service' etc. Same word-boundary concern.
Settings reset uses window.confirm browser dialog — not test-friendly and inconsistent with shadcn design system. Tests work when dialog is auto-accepted.
candidate-row uses data-testid but only the name child <Link> is clickable; UX issue (noted above).

All required data-testid attributes present and working: nav-*, kpi-*, candidate-row-*, filter-*, category-*, decision-*, weight-slider-*, weight-input-*, threshold-input-*, keyword-group-*, keyword-input-*, keyword-add-*, keyword-chip-*, settings-save, settings-reset, settings-saved-banner.
Settings.jsx is 200+ lines but well-organized into sub-components (WeightsTab/ThresholdsTab/KeywordsTab). Acceptable.
Backend correctly returns 404 for missing candidate on GET/PATCH/DELETE.
Upload endpoint correctly rejects unsupported extensions with errors array while still processing valid files in same batch — verified.
test_credentials: N/A - no auth in this app
seed_data_creation: Each test that needs candidates creates synthetic PNG resumes inline with TEST_ filename prefix and deletes them in teardown. Original 6 seeded candidates left untouched. Settings reset to defaults after testing.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend pytest file at /app/backend/tests/test_signalrank.py covers all 11 endpoints + threshold consistency + CRUD persistence. Re-run with: pytest /app/backend/tests/test_signalrank.py -v. Settings reset uses window.confirm; when testing via Playwright, register page.on('dialog', lambda d: asyncio.create_task(d.accept())). Candidate row navigation requires clicking the name <a> link, not the row itself. The 6 seeded candidates currently bucket as Review (2) / Rejected (4) — OCR on synthetic images yields names like 'Prop Shore', 'Ao De' which is expected per problem statement.
rca of the issue: N/A - no failing tests. The one test failure I encountered during initial run (weight not resetting to 30) was due to playwright auto-dismissing the window.confirm dialog. After registering a dialog accept handler, reset works as designed. This is not a bug but a testing nuance worth documenting.
